title: astragalus aquae-rubrae
intro: Astragalus aquae-rubrae, also known as Red Vetch or Red Milk Vetch, is a perennial legume plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It is a member of the pea family and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immune-modulating effects. The health benefits of Astragalus aquae-rubrae include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and fatigue, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory diseases such as bronchitis and asthma. Astragalus aquae-rubrae can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, capsules, and tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
